prompt: Write a dry one-paragraph Amazon review of the experience of waking up.
response: Waking up, while technically included with every mattress purchase, is a persistent and largely unrewarding experience. The product fails to maintain deep sleep modes beyond approximately six hours, initiates abrupt consciousness without meaningful transition, and offers inconsistent ambient lighting—often defaulting to a dull gray or, worse, blinding sunlight. Frequent, uninvited interruptions from internal alarms (brain buzzing at 3 a.m. for no reason) reduce overall satisfaction. Bonus features like “sudden panic over forgotten responsibilities” and “stiff neck acquisition” were not clearly disclosed during checkout. Would not recommend to light sleepers or anyone seeking peace. One star only because staying asleep was not an option.
